translation.en: I have to be on time for work.

notes_md:
**kịp** = to be in time, to make it before it’s too late. It’s about timing — not missing the moment.

It usually goes before a verb or time expression to show you still have enough time to do something.  

Very often used in negative form **không kịp** = not in time / too late to do something.  
It can also mean “keep up with” in speed or ability (không kịp người khác).  

Close idea: **đúng giờ** (on time) — but *kịp* focuses more on “not too late,” not exact punctuality.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This morning I woke up late but still got to school in time.
I try to finish my homework before time runs out so I can submit in time.
We have to go quickly to get to the bus station in time for the last trip.

translation.en: I have to be on time for work.

example.es: Pude llegar a la reunión a tiempo.
example.vi: Tôi đã kịp đến buổi họp.
example.en: I was able to make it to the meeting.

definitions.en: To be in time; to catch up.

notes:
kịp = to be in time, to make it before it’s too late. It’s about timing — not missing the moment.

It usually goes before a verb or time expression to show you still have enough time to do something.  

Very often used in negative form không kịp = not in time / too late to do something.  
It can also mean “keep up with” in speed or ability (không kịp người khác).  

Close idea: đúng giờ (on time) — but kịp focuses more on “not too late,” not exact punctuality.
exampleSentences: Sáng nay anh dậy muộn nhưng vẫn đến trường kịp.
Em cố gắng hoàn thành bài tập trước khi hết giờ để nộp kịp.
Chúng ta phải đi nhanh mới đến bến xe kịp chuyến cuối.
